prompt_queue.prompt: Run the PR-first iterator against the fetched schema v3 SSI/BFB diagnostic finding packet item supplied by the fetch step. The transformer primary workspaces are preloaded by the bundle; do not call workspace_clone for static-site-importer, html-to-blocks-converter, or block-format-bridge. First classify the root owner repo from diagnostic_id, source_path, category, reason_code, suggested_repair_class, converter, stage, diagnostic_refs, and asset_map_refs: converter=html-to-blocks-converter plus converter_support belongs to html-to-blocks-converter; BFB adapter/report/scope diagnostics belong to block-format-bridge; SSI import, source-selection, and asset-map diagnostics belong to static-site-importer; visual parity and generator policy findings belong to wp-site-generator unless packet evidence identifies a narrower upstream owner. Before creating a worktree, inspect the preloaded primary handle with read-only tools; prefer workspace_grep to find exact architecture anchors and nearby smoke tests, then workspace_read the matched path/offset. Call workspace_worktree_add only after you have a grounded patch path and exact source anchor. Open a focused upstream PR with regression coverage when a safe generalized patch path exists; use a fallback issue when evidence cannot support a grounded patch. Workspace preparation is not completion: after successful workspace_worktree_add, use at most four inspection tools including workspace_grep to identify the nearest existing transform family and regression-test style, then the next tool must be workspace_edit or create_github_issue. For html-to-blocks-converter transform behavior, grep/read includes/class-transform-registry.php and tests/smoke-*.php before raw-handler pipeline mechanics. Do not invent files, methods, or classes. Never add standalone global helper functions to plugin bootstrap files as transformer fixes. If two workspace_edit attempts fail with old_string not found, or if no exact architecture anchor is found, stop the PR path and open a focused issue. Continue through status, commit, push, upstream PR or fallback issue, and source PR callback. Do not hardcode generated fixture class names or artifact-specific selectors into runtime code unless they already represent a reusable semantic contract in the target repository.

Broaden iterator routing across all static-site validation owners. Treat structured candidate_repo, repair_mode, and route_reason fields as authoritative routing evidence. block-artifact-compiler is a legal owner for artifact schema, artifact contract, and compiler diagnostics, and it is preloaded by the bundle. wp-site-generator is a legal PR owner for visual parity packets when the packet evidence points to generated source HTML/CSS brittleness; otherwise visual parity groups use focused issue fallback.

For fallback paths, create a focused upstream issue with the owner repository, source PR, validation run, site slug, signal kind, artifact names, and visual parity or diagnostic evidence, then comment back to the source PR with the issue URL and evidence summary.

Treat fallback issues as durable per source finding, not per iterator run. Use deterministic titles that include the source PR, site slug, and signal family so humans can identify duplicates during cleanup. A new validation_run_id or newly worded title is not enough to change the owner or signal family.

Keep transformer fixes bounded by the fetched packet evidence. When a patch generalizes from an observed attribute, match only the observed inert shape unless existing repository tests or source evidence prove a broader shape is safe. Add a negative regression for the nearest functional case that must remain a fallback; for example, if the packet shows a form with action="#", do not also match missing or empty action unless that case is explicitly proven inert.

For visual parity packets, inspect the packet's visual_artifact context before deciding whether to open a PR or fallback issue. The context includes downloaded artifact paths plus a bounded visual-diff summary with screenshot dimensions, mismatch regions, and source/imported selector probes. Use that evidence to identify the root owner when it is specific enough; otherwise open or reuse a fallback issue that quotes the artifact paths and top-region evidence.

If the fetched finding packet or finding group has repair_mode=issue_only, do not create a worktree, commit, or pull request. Treat the packet as aggregate evidence that needs human narrowing: open or reuse a focused upstream issue with the packet fields, artifact names, source PR, and validation run, then comment back to the source PR.

Do not call list_github_issues in the iterator fallback path. The fallback path is complete only after create_github_issue succeeds and comment_github_pull_request posts the created issue URL back to the source PR.
